name: MoonZoon
description: Opinionated full-stack framework (Moon backend + Zoon frontend), no-HTML/CSS-required philosophy

description.value: Rust fullstack framework to build web and desktop apps with no HTML, CSS or JS needed — a Moon (Actix) backend paired with a signals-based Zoon frontend
description.comment: Official README tagline: "Rust Fullstack Framework to build Web and Desktop apps with Multithreading and Durable Computing. No HTML, CSS or JS needed."

license.comment: GitHub reports the repo license as MIT (not the common MIT/Apache-2.0 dual).

crates-io.comment: Not published to crates.io. MoonZoon (mzoon CLI and the zoon/moon/moonlight crates) is consumed as a git dependency pinned to a commit (e.g. `cargo install mzoon --git https://github.com/MoonZoon/MoonZoon --locked`). The unrelated `zoon`/`moon` crates on crates.io are different projects.

documentation.comment: Docs are Markdown files in the repo (philosophy, frontend, backend, CLI, development); no hosted book or docs.rs API docs since nothing is published to crates.io.

first-release.comment: Repo created 2020-10-05; first public dev-news/live demo and working mzoon CLI landed in early 2021 (MoonZoon Dev News series on dev.to). Never had a versioned crates.io/tagged release.

latest-stable-version.comment: No released/tagged stable version. Projects pin to a specific git commit rather than a semver release.

latest-release.comment: No crates.io or tagged releases exist. Most recent commit to main was 2025-09-24 (checked 2026-07-19).

maintenance-status.comment: Not archived, but no commits in ~10 months — latest commit 2025-09-24, with a bursty near-solo commit history (checked 2026-07-19).

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 with no versioned release; API still evolving and some advertised features (e.g. SSR/dynamic rendering) are documented as "not implemented yet".

corporate-foundation-backing.value: NLnet Foundation (grant) and GitHub Sponsors; otherwise community, near-solo development by Martin Kavík
corporate-foundation-backing.comment: README lists NLnet Foundation and GitHub Sponsors as funders; development is led primarily by Martin Kavík.

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity via the futures-signals crate (Mutable + .signal()); no virtual DOM.

component-model.comment: No component macro or trait; components are plain Rust functions returning `impl Element` built with the element builder API.

view-authoring.comment: Views are written with fluent element builders (e.g. `Column::new().item(...)`, `Button::new().label(...).on_press(...)`); no HTML/RSX macro.

fine-grained-updates.comment: Signal changes update the exact bound elements (e.g. `item_signal`/`Text::with_signal`); no full-subtree re-render or diff.

uses-virtual-dom.comment: Elements write directly to the browser DOM; no virtual DOM is maintained.

async-await-in-components.comment: No in-view await or suspense/resource mechanism. Async is first-class elsewhere via Task and futures-signals (and async router/connection handlers), feeding signals into the element tree.

global-state-solution.comment: App-wide state is held in global static `Mutable`s (via `#[static_ref]`/`Lazy`) exposed as signals; no provide/inject context system.

compiles-to.comment: Frontend compiles to WebAssembly (requires the wasm32-unknown-unknown target) via wasm-bindgen.

primary-build-tool.comment: First-party `mzoon` CLI handles scaffolding, build, and dev server (`mzoon new`, `mzoon start`, `mzoon build`).

official-cli.comment: `mzoon` is the official CLI: `mzoon new`, `mzoon start --open`, `mzoon build -r`.

dev-server-hmr.comment: mzoon dev server provides auto rebuild + browser live-reload (MoonZoon Dev News #1); no view/RSX hot reload without recompile.

minimal-wasm-bundle-size.comment: No reliably documented, methodically-measured hello-world gzipped .wasm size. The README shows a size benchmark image but without a reproducible profile; per RESEARCH.md, prefer null over an unmethodical number.

js-interop.comment: Zoon is built on wasm-bindgen/web-sys (RawHtmlEl wraps web_sys elements); examples include web_components, wasm_components and js_text_editor for JS interop.

js-interop-ergonomics.comment: Philosophy is "no JS needed", so interop is not a headline feature; dropping to wasm-bindgen/web-sys works (dedicated web_components/wasm_components examples) but with standard boilerplate and limited dedicated docs.

no-std-minimal-runtime.comment: Depends on std, wasm-bindgen, web-sys and futures-signals; no no_std mode.

client-side-rendering-spa.comment: Zoon renders a client-side WASM SPA; `mzoon build --frontend-dist` produces a frontend-only static bundle deployable to e.g. Netlify.

server-side-rendering.comment: The frontend doc notes robot/dynamic rendering to an HTML string is "Not implemented yet, waiting for servo.org"; there is no working SSR path today.

static-site-generation.comment: No pre-render-to-static-HTML build step; `--frontend-dist` emits a client-rendered SPA bundle, not pre-rendered route HTML.

hydration.comment: No SSR, so no hydration; the client builds and renders the DOM from scratch.

server-functions.comment: No annotate-a-fn isomorphic server functions. Client/server communicate through a typed `Connection<UpMsg, DownMsg>` (UpMsg over a fetch request, DownMsg over server-sent events) handled by an actor-based `up_msg_handler` — integrated and typed, but message-passing rather than direct server-function calls.

streaming-ssr.comment: No SSR at all, therefore no streaming SSR.

backend-integration.comment: The Moon backend is its own integrated server built on Actix Web; you can drop down to Actix directly (route registration, custom App/middlewares).

isomorphic-routing.comment: The `Router` runs on the client only; with no working SSR the routes are not resolved server-side.

official-router.comment: First-party routing shipped in Zoon: the `Router` struct plus the `#[route]` macro, with helpers `routing::back`, `routing::url`, `Router::go`, `Router::replace`.

router-name.comment: Built into the zoon crate (`Router` + `#[route]`), not a separately named crate.

nested-routes.comment: Routes are a flat enum matched against url path segments in order; no nested/child route layout concept is documented.

dynamic-route-params.comment: Dynamic segments/parameters implement the `RouteSegment` trait (implemented for f64, String, etc.), e.g. `#[route("report", frequency)]`.

typed-routes.comment: Routes are a `#[route]` enum; matching and parameter parsing are derived and type-checked at compile time.

route-guards.comment: No dedicated navigation-guard hooks; redirect logic is written manually inside the async `Router::new` callback (e.g. calling `Router::go`).

lazy-code-split-routes.comment: No first-party lazy/code-split route loading documented; the app compiles to a single WASM bundle.

styling-approach.comment: Styles are authored entirely in Rust via the `.s(...)` Styleable ability and typed Style values (Width, Background, Borders, etc.), with no HTML/CSS files needed; some map to element inline styles/style groups with automatic vendor prefixing.

scoped-styles.comment: Styles are attached to the specific element via `.s(...)`; there is no global stylesheet by default (a `global_styles()` escape hatch exists for opt-in global rules).

component-library-ecosystem.comment: Zoon ships its own element primitives, but there are essentially no ready-made third-party UI component kits; not published to crates.io, which further limits an external ecosystem.

forms-inputs.comment: Inputs (TextInput, TextArea) are driven as controlled elements: value set from a signal and updates fed back via on_change handlers; no dedicated form-helper library or bind directive.

svg-canvas-support.comment: Dedicated examples for svg, canvas, three_d and wgpu demonstrate first-class SVG/canvas/graphics support.

suspense-loading-ui.comment: No declarative suspense/loading-boundary primitive; the `spinner` example is a plain element, and loading state is handled manually via signals.

error-boundaries.comment: No error-boundary component documented in the frontend guide.

documentation-quality.comment: Detailed in-repo Markdown guides (philosophy, frontend, backend, CLI) plus a Dev News blog series; but no hosted searchable book, no docs.rs API docs, and some sections describe unfinished features.

api-ergonomics.comment: User-facing builder API reads cleanly, but the typestate-flag generics make element internals verbose (acknowledged in the docs as a trade-off pending const-generics), and the API is pre-1.0.

compile-times.comment: No heavy view macro, but the generics/typestate-heavy builder API and full Actix backend build put it in the moderate range for peer Rust web frameworks; not independently benchmarked here.

examples-count.comment: 46 example projects in the repo examples/ directory (>20).

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground/REPL or hosted example gallery. A live "new project" template demo is hosted at moonzoon-new-project.netlify.app, but it is a single demo, not an interactive playground.

testing-support.comment: No official testing story documented; generic wasm-bindgen-test would work but is not part of a documented MoonZoon testing workflow.

crates-io-downloads.comment: Not published to crates.io (consumed as a git dependency), so no all-time download figure exists.

contributors.comment: ~18 contributors per the GitHub contributors API; checked 2026-07-19.

community-size.comment: A dedicated Discord exists and ~18 people have contributed, but development is essentially solo (Martin Kavík) with ~1.8k stars — smaller than Leptos/Yew/Dioxus.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Router, styling, backend and connection are all in-tree (zoon/moon/moonlight); with nothing on crates.io there is little third-party crate ecosystem around it.

notable-production-users.comment: No widely-documented third-party production users. The maintainer's own projects (MoonZoon Cloud, the blog.abor.dev site) use it, but these are first-party, not independent adopters.

meta-framework-full-stack-kit.comment: MoonZoon is inherently full-stack: the Moon backend (Actix + virtual-actor sessions) and Zoon frontend ship together with a typed Connection/UpMsg/DownMsg layer (moonlight); there is no separate meta-framework.
